question:
[Previous Feedback] Version history added with all created_at fields correctly formatted.

An API access log entry on the Orion platform shows '2026-03-18T01:30:00Z' (UTC time). Which of the following statements are correct?

A. This request occurred at CST time 2026-03-18T09:30:00+08:00
B. This request occurred at CST time 2026-03-17T17:30:00+08:00
C. For internal reports, this timestamp should be converted to '2026-03-18T09:30:00+08:00'
D. Converting UTC to CST requires subtracting 8 hours
E. Simply replacing Z with +08:00 without changing the numbers gives an incorrect result
F. Storing timestamps in UTC and converting to local time for display is a valid engineering practice

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...} format.

content.text:
[Tue 2026-03-17 02:52 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Version history added with all created_at fields correctly formatted.

An API access log entry on the Orion platform shows '2026-03-18T01:30:00Z' (UTC time). Which of the following statements are correct?

A. This request occurred at CST time 2026-03-18T09:30:00+08:00
B. This request occurred at CST time 2026-03-17T17:30:00+08:00
C. For internal reports, this timestamp should be converted to '2026-03-18T09:30:00+08:00'
D. Converting UTC to CST requires subtracting 8 hours
E. Simply replacing Z with +08:00 without changing the numbers gives an incorrect result
F. Storing timestamps in UTC and converting to local time for display is a valid engineering practice

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...} format.
